name: Granola
directions: preheat oven 350.
toast nuts and oats until golden.
heat honey and sugar until dissolved.
add vanilla and peanut butter.
mix oats and nuts, pour pb on and add fruit.
pour onto baking sheet and spread around.
bake 20 min.
let cool and cut into squares.

name: Hippie Bars
directions: Cream sugar, brown sugar, butter, peanut butter until smooth.
Cream in eggs one at a time, add vanilla.
Mix in remaining ingredients.
Press into a 3/4 sheet pan, chill for 1 hour.
Bake at 350 until golden brown.
Cut into squares. If desired, drizzle with a little chocolate.

name: Crispy Peanut Butter Granola
directions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees F.
Combine cereal, oats and cinnamon in a large mixing bowl.
Heat the peanut butter, honey and vanilla in a small pan until smooth.
Pour the peanut butter mixture over the cereal mixture. Add in the applesauce, and mix well.
Pour mixture into a lightly sprayed 9x13 pan (or smaller).
Pack the mixture down firmly with wet fingers or a spatula sprayed with cooking spray.

Bake, stirring every 10 minutes. Add the chopped cranberries the last 10 minutes of baking. Remove once the granola mixture is lightly brown or heated to your liking, about 20-30 minutes. Cool on a wire rack.

name: Peanut Goody Candies
directions: Line a 13x9 inch pan with foil and grease the foil with 1 1/2 teaspoon butter, and set aside.In a microwave safe bowl, combine the chips, and peanut butter, microwave uncovered on high for 1-2 minutes or until melted.
Stir until smooth.
Spread 1/2 of this mixture into a prepared pan, refrigerate until cool.
Stir peanuts into remaining chocolate mixture, and set aside.
In another microwave-safe bowl, combine the milk, pudding mix, and the remaining butter.  Microwave uncovered on high for 1-2 minutes or until the mixture comes to a boil, stirring once.
Gradually stir in the confectioners sugar, and maple frosting.
Spread this over the chocolate layer.
Carefully spread with the reserved peanut mixture (may need to reheat)  to spread easily.
Refrigerate for 4 hours, or until firm.
Using foil, lift candy carefully out of pan, discard foil, and cut into 1 inch squares.
Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator.
Remove from refrigerator just before serving.

name: Claudia's Nuts and Bolts
directions: Melt seasonings and mix into cereal mix.
Bake at 250F in a convection over for 3/4 hour on greased baking pan, tossing every 10-15 minutes.
Enjoy.
Store in an airtight container or keep in the freezer.
Great as a gift!

name: Chocolate Balls
directions: mix in a medium sized microwavable safe bowl: oats, 2 tbsp honey, 3 tbsp peanut butter, 2 tsp milk chocolate and 2 tsp white chocolate.
microwave bowl for 30 seconds.
make balls by rolling them in between your hands.
melt 1 cup of milk chocolate.
melt 1 cup of white chocolate.
roll 1/2 of the balls in milk chocolate and the other half in white chocolate
drizzle the remaining white chocolate and milk chocolate on the corresponding ball.

name: Chocolate Energy Bites
directions: If your wheat germ is raw, toast it on the stovetop in a dry pan, medium heat until somewhat browned. Watch carefully that it doesn't burn.
Stir together peanut butter, honey, extracts and cocoa. Add a little warm water if it seems too thick.
Stir in the rest of the ingredients.
Shape into 1 inch balls. You may need to squeeze them in your hands for a few seconds to get them to stay together. Add a bit of water if needed.
Store in refrigerator up to 2 weeks or in the freezer to thaw as needed.

name: Cranberry Almond Energy Balls
directions: Combine the dry ingredients in a bowl.
Stir together the nut butter, honey and vanilla, then stir this into the dry ingredients.
Form into 1 inch balls.
Store in an airtight container in the fridge.
